title: convolvulus valentinus var. oranensis
intro: Convolvulus valentinus var. oranensis, also known as the Syrian honeysuckle or the orange jay, is a perennial flowering plant in the Convolvulaceae family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Convolvulus valentinus var. oranensis is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
